name: Spotify Web API
what_it_does: Provides access to Spotify's music catalog and user account data, including playlist management, playback control, track/album/artist metadata, search, recommendations, and user listening history.
use_cases: Building music discovery agents that create playlists based on mood or activity
Controlling Spotify playback from smart home or productivity agents
Analyzing user listening habits and generating personalized recommendations
Syncing playlist content across apps or building social music features
Fetching track metadata, audio features, and popularity data for music analytics
not_for: Commercial music services that compete with Spotify
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a user's Spotify account for playback control, playlist management, or music discovery within Spotify's ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need audio file access, offline playback without the Spotify client, or music from catalogs outside Spotify.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with three flows: Authorization Code (user-facing agents), Client Credentials (catalog-only, no user context), and PKCE (mobile/SPA). Scopes are granular per feature area (e.g., playlist-modify-public, user-read-playback-state). Access tokens expire in 1 hour and require refresh token rotation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free developer access to most endpoints; 25 users in development mode without approval
pricing.paid_tiers: No direct API cost; quota extensions require app review. Spotify Premium subscription required for playback control endpoints.
pricing.notes: API itself is free. Apps in development mode are limited to 25 Spotify users until approved for extended access. Playback-related endpoints require the end user to have a Spotify Premium subscription.

performance.rate_limits: Approximate: 180 requests per minute per app. No official published limit; enforced dynamically with 429 responses.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors return HTTP status codes with a JSON body containing error.status and error.message. Messages are human-readable but not always actionable for agents (e.g., 'Player command failed: Not paused' lacks remediation steps).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read endpoints are naturally idempotent. Write endpoints (add to playlist, start playback) are not idempotent and have no idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are undocumented and enforced dynamically - agents must handle 429 with Retry-After header
Access tokens expire in exactly 3600 seconds - agents must implement proactive token refresh before expiry
Playback control endpoints silently fail if user does not have Spotify Premium - returns 403 PREMIUM_REQUIRED
Development mode apps can only have 25 users total until app review approval, blocking production agent deployments
Active device required for playback - if no Spotify client is active, /me/player/play returns 404 NO_ACTIVE_DEVICE
OAuth scopes must be re-requested if new scopes are needed - existing tokens are not upgraded

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on all endpoints. OAuth 2.0 scopes are well-granulated per capability. PKCE flow supported for public clients. Client secrets must be kept server-side. No webhook signature verification (webhooks not supported).
